propositions.text: there's something about clay I keep circling back to when I see it
the accountant asked for the receipts by Friday
I booked a taster session at the pottery studio near the market
I have a weekly wheel slot now and my cylinders finally have even walls
the neighbours' renovation starts at seven every morning
I glazed a full dinner set and we actually eat off it
I'm teaching the beginners' handbuilding class on Saturdays now

questions.q: how has my pottery practice evolved?

propositions.text: I noticed I stop and listen every time I hear a bird I can't name
I downloaded a birdsong ID app and it lives on my home screen
the strata meeting moved to the second Tuesday of the month
I bought proper binoculars and joined the dawn walk group
I recorded forty-one species this spring and submitted my first atlas survey

questions.q: trace how my birdwatching interest developed

propositions.text: supermarket bread has started tasting like packaging to me
I fed a sourdough starter for the first time; it's named Clint
my first two sourdough loaves were bricks; the third had actual holes
the library fines finally cleared after I returned the atlas
I bake every Sunday now and the crumb is consistently open
friends place bread orders when they visit; I baked six loaves for the wedding

questions.q: how has my sourdough baking evolved?

propositions.text: I heard a cello suite live and something in my chest wouldn't settle afterwards
I rented a cello before I could talk myself out of it
the parking permit renewal needs the new lease as proof
open strings only for three weeks; my bow arm is finally quiet
I played the first Bach minuet through without stopping tonight

questions.q: when did I first get into the cello and how has it gone?

propositions.text: I record my podcast every Thursday night in the hallway closet
the flatmate situation changed — Maria moved in with her night-shift schedule
the ficus needs repotting; roots are coming out the bottom
I record the podcast Sunday mornings at the co-working studio now

questions.q: how has my podcast routine changed and why?

propositions.text: I play tennis with Dad every Saturday at the club courts
Dad's hip operation is scheduled for early June
the printer is out of magenta again somehow
Saturdays are now doubles with Dad coaching from the bench, and I hit with the ball machine on Wednesdays

questions.q: how has my tennis routine changed and why?

propositions.text: I write fiction on the 7:12 train every morning; forty minutes of pure focus
the company announced permanent work-from-home starting May
the smoke alarm chirped all night until I found the spare battery
I write in the park cafe from 7 to 8 now — I had to rebuild the train's borrowed discipline somewhere

questions.q: how has my writing routine changed and why?

propositions.text: I see my therapist in person every second Monday
her practice is relocating to the coast at the end of summer
the bins go out Tuesday night now, not Wednesday
we do video sessions weekly now instead of in-person fortnightly — shorter but more frequent works better anyway

questions.q: how has my therapy arrangement changed and why?

propositions.text: we lost the house at auction by fifteen thousand; I'm devastated
I keep driving past the house we lost; it stings every time
the vet says the cat needs a dental next month
the building report on that house came up in conversation — subsidence, the works; losing that auction saved us
the place we bought instead has the garden we didn't know we needed; losing the first house was the luckiest day of the year

questions.q: how has my view of the house auction changed over time?

propositions.text: my thesis supervisor rejected the entire third chapter; six months of work dismissed in one meeting
I can't open the thesis file without hearing that meeting
the gym is closed for resurfacing until the 14th
rereading the supervisor's notes on the thesis chapter — she was protecting me from a reviewer bloodbath
the rewritten thesis chapter is now the paper under review at the journal; her rejection built it

questions.q: how has my view of the thesis rejection changed?

propositions.text: the band broke up over text; ten years ended in three grey bubbles
I haven't touched the bass since the band ended
the council approved the driveway permit at last
hearing our old demos, the band had stopped being fun two years before it ended
I'm writing solo stuff now that sounds like me; the band ending was the door opening

questions.q: trace my thinking about the band breakup

propositions.text: moving countries feels like I've amputated my old life; everything here is beige and hard
I said 'back home' four times in one meeting today; the move still doesn't feel real
the blender's warranty claim finally went through
I caught myself giving directions to a tourist like a local; maybe the move is becoming home
flying 'home' for the holidays and realizing home is now here — the move gave me two homes, not zero

questions.q: how has my feeling about the move changed over the year?

propositions.text: a glass of wine with dinner is one of life's non-negotiable pleasures
I stopped drinking entirely for the marathon block — zero alcohol since February
the letterbox lock jammed again; third time this year
I drink again now but only socially, never at home alone — the break rewrote the habit

questions.q: trace how my relationship with alcohol has gone back and forth

propositions.text: I've gone full minimalist — everything I own fits in the photo I just took of one shelf
minimalism was making the flat feel like a waiting room; I've started keeping books and art again
the ceiling fan wobbles at speed three; needs balancing
I've landed on 'curated, not minimal' — few things, but warm ones; the empty-shelf phase taught me what I actually miss

questions.q: trace how my relationship with minimalism has gone back and forth

propositions.text: paper books only — the smell, the weight; e-readers are soulless slabs
the physio said reading two-kilo hardbacks in bed is what's wrecking my wrist — I bought an e-reader
the recycling collection moved to fortnightly without notice
paper for poetry and anything I'll reread, e-reader for everything else — the wrist made the rule, but it's the right rule

questions.q: trace how my relationship with paper books has gone back and forth

propositions.text: I sold the car; city living means I'll never own one again
car-share worked until the weekend hospital runs for Dad started; I bought a small used hatchback
the succulents survived the heatwave, barely
the hatchback stays, but it lives at Dad's and I still don't drive in the city — car ownership on my own terms this time

questions.q: trace how my relationship with car ownership has gone back and forth
